title: 10 Journal Prompts to Make You Stop Caring What Others Think
intro: Other people's opinions are just noise, not instructions. These prompts are designed to help you treat their words like background noise that you have the power to ignore, allowing you to live exactly how you choose without needing anyone else's permission.
items: Think about the words that are bothering you right now. If those words cannot actually stop you from breathing, moving, or taking your next step, why are you giving them the power to stop your happiness?
Why are you taking directions from a spectator in the stands when you are the one on the field actually living your life?
If someone judges you and thinks you are wrong, what is the worst thing that actually happens to your real life if they just keep thinking that while you keep moving forward?
If you would never go to this specific person for life advice, why are you letting their criticism stay in your head for even one minute?
How much energy do you waste trying to make people 'understand' your choices when you could just let them be wrong and use that energy for yourself instead?

Most people judge others just to feel better about their own insecurities. When you realize their comment is actually about their own mess and has nothing to do with you, how much easier is it to just walk away?
Imagine you have a 'mute' button for other people's opinions. What is the very first thing you would do today if you pushed that button and stopped listening to everyone else?
You are the only person who has to live with your choices at the end of the day, so why are you letting people who don't live your life decide how you should feel?

Think of a time someone judged you a year ago. Do you even remember exactly what they said, or has that noise completely disappeared? Why give today's noise more importance than it will have in the future?
Write down the one thing you are most afraid people will say about you. If they said it to your face today and you just walked away without answering, how much power would you take back?
